# Roadmap — rhino-os

## Vision

Make Claude Code intelligent for solo founders through AI evals.

Raw Claude Code is a powerful code assistant but a dumb one — it has no memory, no measurement, no learning loops. rhino-os turns it into a system that knows what's working, remembers what failed, and tells you what to build next. The core insight: **evals aren't just for testing — they're the intelligence layer that makes every AI session smarter than the last.**

The end state: a solo founder installs rhino-os, runs `/setup`, and from that point on every Claude Code session has context (what happened last time), measurement (is the product getting better), and direction (what to build next). The AI stops being a tool you prompt and starts being a system that thinks.

## Current State (v3.0)

- 19 skills covering the full build loop
- Two-tier scoring (structural + visual)
- Experiment tracking with learning accumulation
- 6 agent brains with persistent memory
- Hook system for session context injection
- Multi-project workspace management
- Self-eval test suite (242/251 passing)
- Integrity enforcement (anti-sycophancy, anti-gaming)

## What's Next

### Near-term (sharpen the loop)
- Thinking protocol adoption — predictions.tsv is empty, agents aren't following it yet
- Agent value audit — which of the 19 skills actually produce value vs. noise
- Loop compounding proof — demonstrate that experiment learnings from cycle N appear in cycle N+1
- Onboarding friction — what breaks first for a new user running /setup on their project

### Medium-term (distribution)
- One-command install that works on any project type
- README / docs that explain the value prop in 30 seconds
- GitHub stars / community — solo founders finding this organically
- Example projects showing before/after with rhino-os

### Long-term (the eval platform)
- Custom eval specs per project (TDD for AI — already started)
- Eval marketplace or library (share eval patterns across projects)
- Taste eval that works without Playwright (cheaper, faster, more accessible)
- Score dimensions that learn from the project (not just grep patterns)

## What We Are NOT Building

- **GUI / web dashboard** — The terminal is the interface. Files are the database. No Electron app, no localhost UI, no hosted dashboard.
- **Multi-user / team features** — This is for one person. No permissions, no shared state, no collaboration.
- **Hosted service / SaaS** — Everything runs locally. No accounts, no cloud, no API keys to manage.
- **IDE plugin** — This is Claude Code native. Not VS Code, not Cursor, not any other editor.
- **General-purpose AI agent framework** — This is specifically for solo founders building startups. Not a generic agent orchestrator.
- **LLM-as-judge scoring** — Deterministic measurement wherever possible. Vision eval (taste.mjs) is the exception, and it has integrity guards.

## Kill Criteria

What would make this project not worth continuing:
- Claude Code removes hooks or skill system (platform risk)
- Solo founders stop using Claude Code in favor of something else
- The eval loop demonstrably doesn't compound (each cycle independent, no learning)
- Someone builds this better and open-sources it

## Priorities (ordered)

1. **Prove the loop compounds** — This is the thesis. If it doesn't work, nothing else matters.
2. **Reduce onboarding friction** — New user → value in <5 minutes
3. **Sharpen measurement** — Score and taste need to be trustworthy signals, not vanity metrics
4. **Distribution** — Get this in front of solo founders who need it
